activities_projects.title: Poem Reading Activity
activities_projects.objective: Understand the main story of the poem
steps: Read the poem carefully
Identify the main characters (Saint Peter and the woman)
Write in 3-4 sentences what the poem is about
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the main story and characters

activities_projects.title: Character Identification
activities_projects.objective: Recognize key characters in the poem
steps: Write the names of the main characters
Describe one quality of each character
Share your description in class

activities_projects.title: Moral of the Story
activities_projects.objective: Understand the lesson conveyed by the poem
steps: Read the poem carefully
Write the moral in 2-3 sentences
Explain why the woman was turned into a woodpecker
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the theme of selfishness and consequences

activities_projects.title: Story Sequence Activity
activities_projects.objective: Understand the order of events in the poem
steps: Write key events from the poem
Arrange them in the correct order
Check your sequence with the textbook

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary Activity
activities_projects.objective: Learn new words from the poem
steps: Find words like hearth, scanty, provoke, bore
Write their meanings
Use them in simple sentences

activities_projects.title: Drawing the Scene
activities_projects.objective: Visualize key events in the poem
steps: Draw the woman baking cakes or turning into a woodpecker
Label important elements in the picture
Share your drawing with classmates

activities_projects.title: Feelings Activity
steps: Write how Saint Peter feels when the woman refuses to give food
Write how the woman behaves and why
Explain in a few sentences

activities_projects.title: Reading Aloud Activity
steps: Practice reading a stanza aloud
Focus on correct pronunciation and pauses
Read it aloud in class

activities_projects.title: Creative Writing
steps: Write a short paragraph about the woodpecker’s life after being transformed
Include details about what she eats and where she lives

activities_projects.title: Moral Reflection
activities_projects.objective: Connect the poem’s lesson to real life
steps: Think of an example where sharing is important in your life
Write 3-4 sentences explaining it
Discuss with classmates
